description: A designed 'news card' style graphic combining a photograph with a captioned headline banner. The upper two-thirds is a color photograph taken inside an industrial warehouse/training facility: a woman in a green blazer and white hard hat with safety glasses (apparently Secretary of Labor) is shaking hands with workers/apprentices, several of whom wear numbered hard hats (38, 51, 61, etc.), safety glasses, and gray work shirts. Lifting equipment, pallet racking, and scaffolding fill the background. A faint embossed U.S. Department of Labor seal is overlaid in the lower-right corner. Across the bottom is a navy/blue label tab reading 'Labor News' in white italic serif, with a few vertical accent bars, above a bold white serif headline on a dark band: 'US Department of Labor launches landmark initiative to integrate artificial intelligence skills into Registered Apprenticeships nationwide.' The lower-right reads 'DOL.GOV'. The messaging is positive promotional/PR framing of a Trump-administration workforce initiative (AI skills in apprenticeships), projecting hands-on engagement with American workers. Target audience: general public, workers, and political supporters.
notable_text: Labor News / US Department of Labor launches landmark initiative to integrate artificial intelligence skills into Registered Apprenticeships nationwide / DOL.GOV
